title: The Subnautica / Krafton story gets even messier.
description:
In the latest update, a judge has ordered Krafton to reinstate former Unknown Worlds Entertainment CEO Ted Gill, saying the publisher violated its contract when Gill and his fellow co-founders were fired last summer. The suit also claims that Krafton CEO Changhan Kim “consulted an artificial intelligence chatbot to contrive a corporate ‘takeover’ strategy” and get out of paying a $250 million bonus.
[Link: Judge Says Krafton Must Rehire Fired ‘Subnautica’ CEO | https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-16/judge-says-krafton-must-rehire-fired-subnautica-ceo | Bloomberg]
content:

title: Nvidia Debuts New A.I. Product at GTC Developer Conference
description: At the opening of the company’s annual conference, Jensen Huang leaned on technology from a recent deal to show how artificial intelligence is changing.
content:

title: US appeals court fines lawyers $30,000 in latest AI-related sanction
description: An appeal containing fake case citations that misrepresent the law can be dismissed as frivolous, a U.S. federal appeals court panel said in a decision sanctioning two attorneys who submitted ​filings that bore hallmarks of artificial intelligence "hallucinations."

title: Why Bigger Isn’t Always Better in AI
description: For years, artificial intelligence has been governed by a simple creed: Bigger is better. Feed a model more data, more chips and more electricity, and it will become smarter.
content:
For years, artificial intelligence has been governed by a simple creed: Bigger is better. Feed a model more data, more chips and more electricity, and it will become smarter.

title: AI moves to the frontline as India backs killer robots, drone swarms and cyberwarfare
description: As the landscape of warfare evolves, India's military is pioneering the use of artificial intelligence to redefine battle tactics.
